While building projects, one thing I noticed is how much time developers spend just finding small mistakes — missing colons, indentation errors, bad practices, and debugging simple issues that could easily be automated.
That’s what led me to build this project:
An AI-powered Code Reviewer
The Idea
Instead of manually checking code or searching errors online, why not let AI do a first-level review instantly?
This system acts like a mini code assistant that reviews your code and gives feedback in real-time.
Problem Statement
Developers and programming learners often face difficulty in identifying errors and improving code quality while writing programs. Traditional code editors only highlight basic syntax errors but do not provide meaningful explanations, suggestions, or guidance for better coding practices.
As a result, beginners spend a lot of time debugging simple issues, searching for solutions online, and switching between different resources, which reduces productivity and slows down the learning process.
There is a need for an intelligent system that can analyze code in real-time and provide clear feedback, error detection, and improvement suggestions to help developers write better and more efficient code.

• Manual code debugging takes time

• Beginners struggle with errors

• No instant feedback system in basic editors
Solution We Built
To solve the problem of slow debugging and lack of real-time guidance for developers, we built an AI-powered Code Reviewer Web Application.
This system allows users to paste their code into a simple web interface, which is then sent to a Flask backend. The backend connects to an AI model that analyzes the code and returns instant feedback.
The AI provides:

• Error detection (syntax and logical issues)

• Code improvement suggestions

• Best practice recommendations

• Simple explanations of the code
The response is displayed instantly in the browser, making it easy for users to understand and fix their code without switching to external resources.
Objectives of the Project

• Automate Code Review

• Provide Instant Feedback

• Improve Learning for Beginners

• Reduce Debugging Time

fig 1: If there is any error in code then it shows that what is an error and how to fix it.Architecture
Press enter or click to view image in full size

The application follows a simple client–server architecture integrated with an AI service layer.
Frontend:

• HTML

• CSS

• JavaScript
The frontend provides a simple user interface where users can paste their code and submit it for review. It sends requests to the backend and displays the AI response in the browser.
Backend:

• Python (Flask)
The backend handles incoming requests from the frontend. It receives the code, processes it, and communicates with the AI service to get code analysis results.
AI Layer:

• OpenRouter / Gemini / GPT-based API
The AI model analyzes the submitted code and generates:

• Error detection

• Code improvement suggestions

• Explanations

• Best practices
Communication:

• REST APIs

• JSON format
The frontend sends a POST request containing the code to the Flask backend. The backend forwards this request to the AI API. The AI response is received in JSON format and sent back to the frontend for display.
Implementation
The frontend of the application is designed with a simple interface that includes a text area for entering code, a button to submit the code for review, and a display section to show the AI-generated feedback.
On the backend side, a Flask application is developed with a /review API endpoint. This endpoint accepts the user’s code as input, processes the request, and sends it to the AI model for analysis. The response received from the AI is then returned back to the frontend.
To ensure smooth communication between the frontend and backend, Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) is enabled, and data exchange is handled using JSON format through RESTful API requests.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

This architecture keeps the system lightweight, easy to understand, and easy to extend in the future.

fig5Challenges We Faced
During the development of this project, we faced several technical and integration challenges that helped us learn and improve the system.
1.Frontend–Backend Communication Issues
Initially, connecting the HTML frontend with the Flask backend was challenging due to incorrect API endpoints and request handling errors. Proper configuration of REST API calls was required to resolve this.
2.CORS Policy Errors
While sending requests from the frontend to the backend, we encountered Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) issues. This was solved by enabling CORS support in the Flask application.
3.Debugging Runtime Errors
We faced runtime errors such as incorrect endpoints, JSON parsing issues, and occasional backend crashes. These were resolved through systematic debugging and logging.
4.Latency in AI Response
Sometimes the AI response took time due to network/API delays, which affected real-time experience. This highlighted the importance of optimizing API calls.
Current Progress
The AI Code Reviewer project has been successfully developed with the core functionalities implemented and working.
Currently, the system includes a fully functional frontend and backend integration. The frontend provides a simple user interface where users can input their code and receive AI-generated feedback instantly.
The backend is built using Flask, which handles API requests and communicates with an AI model to analyze the submitted code. The AI successfully returns suggestions, error detection, and improvements based on the input code.
At present, the system supports real-time code review through REST API communication and JSON data exchange between frontend and backend.
Basic testing has been completed using sample Python and JavaScript code, and the system is able to generate meaningful responses from the AI model.
What We Learned
During the development of this project, we gained practical experience in full-stack web development and AI integration.
We learned how to build a complete application by connecting a frontend interface with a backend server using Flask. We also understood how REST APIs work and how data is exchanged using JSON format.
Another important learning was integrating AI models through APIs, handling responses, and displaying meaningful output to users in real time.
Additionally, we improved our debugging skills by resolving issues related to API calls, CORS configuration, and backend communication.
Future Scope
The project can be further enhanced with several advanced features to improve its functionality and user experience.

• Support for multiple programming languages with better accuracy

• User authentication system to save code history

• Database integration to store previous code reviews

• Real-time collaborative coding and review system

• Advanced AI analysis including performance optimization suggestions

• Deployment of the application as a cloud-based service
These improvements can make the system more powerful and suitable for professional development environments.
Conclusion
The AI Code Reviewer project successfully demonstrates how artificial intelligence can be used to assist developers in improving code quality and reducing debugging time.
By combining Flask for backend development and AI API integration, the system provides real-time feedback on user-submitted code in a simple and efficient manner.
This project shows the potential of AI in enhancing developer productivity and making coding more accessible for beginners and professionals alike.
This system acts like a mini code assistant that reviews your code and gives feedback in real-time.
